name: Hiking along the Ara River — Lacort and Albella, villages of Sobrarbe
name_original: Fiscal por el Río Ara — Lacort y Albella, pueblos del Sobrarbe
region_intro: Hiking around Fiscal, nestled in the Aragonese Pyrenees, offers diverse trails through mountainous and hilly landscapes. The region features significant elevation changes and picturesque views along river valleys, with many routes leading through dense woodlands and open areas. Its proximity to Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park provides access to high mountain topography, deeply carved canyons, and glacial valleys. Best hiking trails around Fiscal The most popular hiking route is Punta Corona – Punta Corona loop from Fiscal, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Viewpoint Over the Ara River – Moscarale Bridge loop from Boltaña, a difficult 12.0 miles (19.3 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views and follows the course of the Ara River. Local hikers also love the Overgrown Trail loop from Fiscal, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through varied paths and woodlands, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Fiscal is defined by mountainous regions, river valleys, and diverse terrain within the Pyrenees. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Fiscal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,400 reviews. More than 8,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Fiscal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Fiscal?
A: Fiscal offers a wide variety of hiking options, with over 160 trails recorded. These routes cater to all skill levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-hour treks through the Aragonese Pyrenees.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Fiscal?
A: Hiking around Fiscal provides access to diverse landscapes, including high mountain topography, deeply carved canyons, and glacial valleys. You'll encounter lush forests of Scots pine, black pine, fir, and beech trees, alongside the Arazas River, and enjoy stunning panoramic views of peaks like Monte Perdido.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Fiscal?
A: Yes, Fiscal has over 30 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes often feature gentler terrain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the region. For example, the area around the Ordesa Meadow offers accessible paths within the National Park.

Q: Can I find challenging multi-day treks or difficult routes around Fiscal?
A: Absolutely. Fiscal is an excellent base for challenging hikes, with over 30 difficult routes available. These trails often involve significant elevation gain and varied terrain, leading to high mountain areas and offering spectacular views. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Corona – Punta Corona loop from Fiscal&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.4 miles (15.2 km) with substantial ascent.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available near Fiscal?
A: Many of the trails around Fiscal are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. This includes popular options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19885811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Overgrown Trail loop from Fiscal&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied paths and woodlands.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or impressive canyons near Fiscal?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its dramatic geological features. You can find trails leading to spectacular waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/322882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soaso Steps Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/617214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also home to magnificent canyons such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/198965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ordesa Canyon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2441602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soaso Cirque&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover on hikes around Fiscal?
A: Hikes around Fiscal offer numerous opportunities to discover impressive natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can reach mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/971762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Cotefablo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5823771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of La Cuta&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic vistas from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6290296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuello Arenas&lt;/a&gt;. The entire Ordesa Valley, with Monte Perdido as its backdrop, is a landmark in itself.

Q: Is Fiscal a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: While dogs are generally welcome on many trails in the broader Aragonese Pyrenees, specific regulations apply within the Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park. It's essential to check the park's official guidelines regarding pet access, especially concerning protected areas and wildlife. Always keep dogs on a leash and ensure they do not disturb local fauna.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fiscal?
A: The best time for hiking in Fiscal is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild, trails are clear of snow, and wildflowers bloom in early summer. Autumn offers stunning foliage. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience due to snow and ice conditions.

Q: Are there parking facilities available at trailheads around Fiscal?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads and access points around Fiscal, especially those leading into Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park, offer designated parking facilities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2866216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ordesa Meadow&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known parking area for accessing trails within the park. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season as spaces can fill up quickly.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Fiscal?
A: The trails around Fiscal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,400 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the variety of landscapes, and the well-maintained paths that offer both challenging ascents and serene walks along river valleys.

Q: Where can I find more information about the Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park?
A: For comprehensive information about the Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park, including regulations, visitor centers, and detailed maps, you can visit the official Spain.info page dedicated to the park: &lt;a href="https://www.spain.info/en/nature/ordesa-monte-perdido-national-park/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The old town of Lacort is a small town that is now uninhabited and is next to the Eix Pirinenc road. You can still see the facades of houses typical of this area of the Pyrenees.
Suspension bridge suitable for pedestrians and bicycles that connects the two sides of the Ara river. It has the name of the old town of Lacort

Albella is a small village that is located below the Sierra del Galardón and where, as a characteristic of the town, there are two churches (one of them a hermitage) for a number of 16 inhabitants. It shows Iglesia de San Salvador de Albella and Ermita de San Úrbez de Albella.
